mnemonics: Methods are the blueprint for the class, and are used to create the functionality of the class.
Constructors are special methods that are automatically called when an object is created, and are used to initialize the object.
keyPoints: Inheritance: A class can inherit properties and methods from another class using the 'this' keyword.
Constructors are used to initialize objects and are called when an object is created.
Garbage Collection: The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) automatically frees up memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use.
